violations.text: Deli meat in the 3-door low boy was observed not marked with the date of opening. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Food item was date marked during the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2025

A bar shaker was observed being used as an ice scoop in the ice well in the bar area. The bar shaker was removed from ice well during the time of inspection and was replaced by a ice scoop. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the refrigerator door gaskets and hood system have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces of the equipment shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to prevent the accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2025

The floor in the ice cream area is missing floor tiles with the subflooring exposed and was observed to be in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2025

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was (00) parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be (200-400) ppm. Chlorine dish washer at bar was reading (00ppm). Establishment shall use dish machine in kitchen and call for service. New Violation. Correct By: 07/14/2023

Ambi tempature of cold hold unit was observed held at 52 degrees Fahrenheit. Food items-lobster, precooked potatoes, sauces and chowder were observed between 47-52 degrees. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Items were voluntarily disposed of. Item were removed from unit and unit recover to an Ambi temp of 37. Establishment shall not overload unit and shall monitor tempature of unit. New Violation. Correct By: 07/14/2023

Cheese, sauces, pico, guac and other various items were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Items were labeled and dated at time of inspection . Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/14/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the hood system, wall by fry station and flooring under food prep sink were observed to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Establishment shall clean and maintain these areas. New Violation. Correct By: 07/14/2023
